gold_change_reason: Operators D and C modify input data complexity and require specific method parameters. The seed solution with default parameters would not satisfy the scaled oracle case.
covered_requirements: Increase input data complexity by using non-Gaussian distributions and different sample sizes to test robustness of the BAR implementation.
Require the user to specify specific BAR method parameters (method='self-consistent-iteration' and uncertainty_method='MBAR') to test understanding of parameter options and numerical handling.
implementation_notes: The code includes the full bar function definition as provided in the seed, ensuring numerical stability with logsumexp and logaddexp.
The main function generates non-Gaussian (exponential) data with different sample sizes and calls bar with the required parameters.
The bar function is called with method='self-consistent-iteration' and uncertainty_method='MBAR' as mandated by the C-axis operator.
assumptions: The bar function implementation is correct and numerically stable for the given data distributions.
